findings.criterion: B-5-1 (add single product updates total) is exercised by at least one scenario

evidence.quote: Then the total price displayed in the element with id "allMoney" should be "$40.00"

findings.reasoning: Scenario E2ESD_Bench_01::5::1 explicitly asserts the #allMoney element shows the formatted single-product total, matching the behaviour contract's expected observable.

findings.criterion: B-5-2 (sum multiple products updates total) is exercised by at least one scenario

evidence.quote: Then the total price displayed in the element with id "allMoney" should be "$160.00"

The system calculates the total price by summing the prices of all products in the cart, formatting the total to two decimal places with the appropriate currency symbol (e.g., '$80.00'), and displays the total in the <div id="allMoney"> element.

findings.reasoning: Scenario E2ESD_Bench_01::5::2 asserts the summed total for multiple products in #allMoney, directly matching the contract that totals are summed and formatted.

findings.criterion: B-5-3 (repeated drops increment quantity and affect total) is exercised by at least one scenario

evidence.quote:
When the user drags the product with data-testid "product-item-3" to the drop area with data-testid "drop-area"
    And the user drags the product with data-testid "product-item-3" to the drop area with data-testid "drop-area"
    Then the total price displayed in the element with id "allMoney" should be "$70.00"
If the user drops the same product into the cart multiple times, the system increments the quantity of that product in the cart.

findings.reasoning: Scenario E2ESD_Bench_01::5::3 performs repeated drops of the same product and asserts the expected total—covering the increment-quantity behaviour and its effect on #allMoney.

findings.criterion: B-5-4 (real-time total update on each add) is validated by the scenario suite

evidence.quote: The user can see the total cost update in real-time as they add more products to the cart.
Then the total price displayed in the element with id "allMoney" should be "$240.00"

findings.reasoning: The behaviour contract requires observing the total update immediately after each add. All provided scenarios assert only final #allMoney values after sequences of adds (e.g., final "$160.00" or "$240.00") and do not assert intermediate updates 'after each drop'. No scenario explicitly checks the UI immediately after each individual drag-and-drop action.
findings.suggested_fix: Add or amend scenarios to assert the #allMoney value immediately after each drag-and-drop step (e.g., after first drop expect $X.XX, after second drop expect $Y.YY) to prove real-time updates.
